status_scale.satisfied: Completion evidence exists and is machine-checkable where practical.
status_scale.partial: Useful repo-side work exists, but the final requirement is not proven.
status_scale.pending_external_evidence: The repo has the harness or process, but real external evidence is still required.
status_scale.deferred_by_user: The requirement is intentionally held until explicit user approval.
status_scale.not_started: No meaningful implementation or validation evidence exists yet.

gates.requirement: Run an API-key-gated OpenAI Responses live smoke and keep only sanitized evidence.
required_evidence: Sanitized live smoke JSON with scenario openai_responses and status passed.
Product-truth register entries for any non-passing OpenAI Responses findings.

current_evidence.proves: A sanitized OpenAI Responses live smoke passed with raw responses omitted.
The reviewed smoke evidence includes scenario openai_responses with status passed.
The complete reviewed live smoke bundle includes OpenAI Responses with status passed.
The remaining OpenAI Responses live-smoke gap requires operator-provided OpenAI credentials.
The live harness skips safely when OPENAI_API_KEY is absent; it does not prove live provider behavior.
The optional live smoke entrypoint exists and validates sanitized report shape before writing output.
Credential and optional dependency readiness can be checked with sanitized next-action guidance and without emitting secret values.
Alpha smoke and preflight evidence share a documented sanitized report contract with mode-specific aggregate smoke, framework, preflight, and sample-price acknowledgement rules.
Alpha smoke evidence contract checks reject forbidden keys, secret-like values, raw responses, summary drift, and schema/checker drift.

gates.remaining_action: Watch future OpenAI Responses live smoke findings and convert any non-passing result into product truth.

gates.requirement: Validate a multi-provider custom-discount case in the alpha smoke harness.
required_evidence: Smoke evidence showing a multi-provider discount case in the same review set as at least one live provider run.
Fixture or documented limitation for any discount behavior discrepancy.

current_evidence.proves: The multi-provider discount case passed in the same reviewed report as live Anthropic and OpenRouter provider scenarios.
The reviewed smoke evidence includes scenario multi_provider_discount with status passed.
The deterministic no-network sample covers the multi-provider discount scenario.
CI verifies the sample multi-provider discount smoke shape and sanitized framework child-failure capture.

gates.requirement: Every live smoke finding becomes a fixture, structured warning, documented limitation, extractor/source fix, or price-source update.
required_evidence: Product-truth register entries matching every non-passing item in the reviewed live smoke report.
Referenced artifacts exist for every non-none classification.

current_evidence.proves: The complete reviewed live smoke bundle has no non-passing smoke findings to convert into additional product-truth artifacts.
The live smoke review records all six scenarios as passed and keeps the remaining billing/export limitation separate.
The product-truth loop is complete for available live evidence and remains dependent on operator-provided missing live/invoice evidence.
The product-truth loop is machine-checkable for current no-credential findings.
The product-truth register has a documented schema-backed contract for status, classification, and artifact-kind compatibility.
Live smoke reports can be checked against the register, and non-passing findings must resolve to a compatible product-truth artifact.
Sanitized smoke findings can be converted into schema-valid product-truth register entries without hand-authoring the JSON fields.
The reviewed Milestone 8 evidence set can be checked as one bundle with required smoke scenarios, product-truth classifications, sample-price acknowledgement, and invoice comparison evidence.
A sanitized evidence bundle collector can create the reviewed artifact directory shape and run the bundle checker across aggregate smoke, Vercel, LangChain, and invoice comparison artifacts.
The sample-mode evidence bundle collector is exercised in CI without credentials and rejects leakage of secret-like values, timestamps, or local ledger paths.
Framework child-script failures become sanitized needs_product_truth findings instead of raw aggregate-runner crashes.
The completion gate register itself has a documented schema-backed contract.

gates.remaining_action: Add product-truth entries for any future non-passing live smoke findings.

gates.requirement: Compare at least one real provider dashboard, invoice, or usage export against RunCost output.
required_evidence: Sanitized comparison JSON with evidence_type real_provider_export.
Comparison passes the --require-real check and documents exact, estimated, and unsupported fields.

current_evidence.proves: A billing/export review packet exists for operator dashboard comparison against the live smoke window.
The review packet lists provider, surface, model, expected request count, and fields to inspect in provider billing or usage exports.
The remaining invoice/dashboard comparison gap requires operator-provided provider billing or usage-export evidence.

The real-evidence check exists, rejects sanitized samples as completion evidence, requires sanitized classified rows with product-truth actions for discrepancies, and verifies unsafe comparison inputs do not produce output.
The comparison producer rejects unsafe/private/secret-like input before writing output.
A sanitized OpenAI Costs API page can be reduced into the generic invoice/dashboard comparison input contract.
An API-key-gated OpenAI Costs API runner can generate sanitized comparison artifacts in sample mode or live mode with OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY and a supplied RunCost ledger.

The sanitized live preflight includes OpenAI Costs invoice comparison readiness, requiring OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY plus the openai_costs_start_time and openai_costs_runcost_ledger input names without printing values or local paths.
A sanitized OpenAI Costs API sample exercises the provider-reported cost comparison-input path without private billing exports.
Invoice/dashboard comparison outputs share a documented sanitized comparison contract.
Sanitized provider dashboard/export reductions have a documented input contract before comparison output is written.
Invoice/dashboard comparison contract checks reject invalid sanitized inputs, forbidden keys, secret-like values, private export flags, summary drift, and discrepancies without product-truth actions.
A sanitized sample proves the workflow shape, not real invoice completion.
The real comparison must pass together with all required smoke evidence before Milestone 8 can close.

gates.remaining_action: Run the comparison against a sanitized real provider dashboard, invoice, or usage export.

gates.requirement: Configure PyPI trusted publishing for adamallcock/runcost and the release environment.
required_evidence: Registry-side PyPI trusted-publisher settings are configured for .github/workflows/release.yml and environment release.
A release workflow run reaches the PyPI publish step without requiring a stored API token when publishing is approved.

current_evidence.proves: The remaining PyPI trusted-publishing gap requires operator-side registry configuration.
The required PyPI trusted-publisher settings are documented.
The workflow requests id-token permissions, uses PyPI trusted publishing, and requires typed publish approval before publish=true can proceed.
Release readiness checks reject stored PyPI/npm registry-token dependencies in the release workflow.
Registry-side trusted publisher verification has a sanitized machine-readable evidence contract.
Trusted-publishing verification checks validate template evidence in CI and provide a strict real-evidence mode for public beta.
The current checked-in trusted-publishing artifact is an explicit template, not real registry setup evidence.
Live registry checks showed PyPI `runcost` is occupied and `runcost-ai` had no matching distribution, so the Python distribution target is `runcost-ai`.
The Python distribution-name decision is recorded: publish `runcost-ai`, while preserving import package and CLI name `runcost`.
The Python package metadata uses distribution name `runcost-ai`.

gates.completion_check: python3 scripts/check_trusted_publishing_verification.py --verification <sanitized-manual-verification.json> --require-real plus guarded release workflow evidence.
gates.remaining_action: Configure and verify the PyPI trusted publisher for project `runcost-ai` outside the repository.

gates.requirement: Run the guarded release workflow with publishing disabled for the real intended beta version.
required_evidence: Successful GitHub release workflow run for the intended beta version with publish=false.
Artifact review checklist has been reviewed for that run.

current_evidence.proves: A publish=false rehearsal succeeded for version 0.0.0, but not for the real intended beta version.
A publish=false guarded release workflow rehearsal succeeded for real intended beta version 0.1.0, artifacts were reviewed, and publishing stayed disabled.

gates.completion_check: gh workflow run release.yml --ref main -f version=<real-version> -f publish=false
gates.remaining_action: Keep publishing disabled until trusted publisher setup and explicit approval; real Go tag verification remains tracked separately.

gates.requirement: Verify the Go module from a real semantic version tag without a local replace.
required_evidence: A pushed semantic version tag exists.
Clean external module can run go get github.com/adamallcock/runcost/packages/go/ledger@v<version> without replace.

current_evidence.proves: The real Go tag verification command is documented.
The release workflow verifies the real tag path when the tag exists.
Remote tag v0.1.0 was verified from a clean Go module without a local replace in guarded release workflow run 26467656634.

gates.remaining_action: Keep using real tag verification for future release versions.

gates.requirement: Publish Python and JavaScript packages after explicit artifact review and approval.
required_evidence: Explicit user approval to publish.
Workflow dispatch includes publish=true and publish_approval=publish-runcost.
PyPI and npm package pages for the released version.
Post-publish install smoke evidence.
gates.completion_check: Guarded release workflow with publish=true and publish_approval=publish-runcost after explicit approval.
gates.deferred_reason: The current objective says to leave packages unpublished for now.
gates.remaining_action: Do not publish until the user explicitly approves publication.
